evidence: Package layout (api, config, query, model, indexer, tagging, utils, viz) maps cleanly to domain capabilities with no obvious historical accidents.
model/ package owns all domain DTOs (AdjectiveProfile, ComparisonResult, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, Edge, ExplorationResult, ExploreOptions) with no cross-cutting concerns.
Handler split into SketchHandlers and ExplorationHandlers within the api package shows intentional grouping by feature area.
QueryExecutor interface provides a clean abstraction boundary between the API layer and the BlackLab backend.

CLAUDE.md code structure section lists a non-existent grammar/ package (CQLParser.java, CQLPattern.java, CQLParseException.java) and wrong package for PosGroup (says query/PosGroup.java but it lives in utils/PosGroup.java). The entire model/ package is absent from the listing, as are api/SketchHandlers.java, api/ExplorationHandlers.java, config/RelationType.java, utils/CqlUtils.java, and utils/PatternSubstitution.java.
api/PatternSubstitution.java is a deprecated wrapper-only class that still resides in the api package (which is otherwise pure HTTP handlers), blurring what the api package owns.

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti() directly calls executor.executeSurfacePattern() and manually builds set intersection logic, bypassing SemanticFieldExplorer. SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles() covers the analogous single-vs-multi comparison for /api/semantic-field, but the /api/semantic-field/explore-multi endpoint does not route through SemanticFieldExplorer at all.

README (line 119) lists GET /api/bcql but the server registers it via server.createContext (not registerGetHandler) and the startup log prints POST /api/bcql, making the public endpoint contract documentation inconsistent.
SemanticFieldExplorer Javadoc references @link #compareSeeds but no such method exists; only compareCollocateProfiles exists.

strengths: Package decomposition is domain-aligned: api, config, query, model, indexer, tagging, utils, viz each map to a recognisable capability.
A dedicated model/ package cleanly owns all domain DTOs with no cross-cutting responsibilities.
QueryExecutor interface provides a single, well-documented contract that separates the HTTP layer from the corpus backend.
Handler classes (SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers) partition the growing API surface by feature area, making the change boundary predictable.
high_level_elegance.issue_character: The primary issues are documentation drift (the canonical architecture reference in CLAUDE.md describes a codebase that no longer exists) and a domain-logic split for multi-seed exploration that places business logic directly in the HTTP handler layer rather than in the dedicated SemanticFieldExplorer service.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: Structurally the codebase is well above average: packages are named after domain concepts, there is a proper model layer, and the query abstraction is clean. However, the high_level_elegance rubric explicitly requires that 'project contracts and reference docs match runtime reality' — and CLAUDE.md's code-structure map is substantially wrong (fictitious grammar/ package, missing model/ package, wrong class locations). Additionally, multi-seed exploration logic is split across ExplorationHandlers (inlined set intersection) and SemanticFieldExplorer (used only for single-seed), violating subsystem localization. These two issues — untrustworthy architecture docs and a fragmented domain capability — pull the score into the mid-50s despite the sound skeletal structure.

issues.summary: CLAUDE.md code-structure map describes a fictitious grammar/ package and omits the actual model/ package and several real classes
evidence: CLAUDE.md lists grammar/CQLParser.java, grammar/CQLPattern.java, grammar/CQLParseException.java — no grammar/ package exists on disk.
CLAUDE.md lists query/PosGroup.java — PosGroup is actually at utils/PosGroup.java.
CLAUDE.md omits the entire model/ package (AdjectiveProfile, ComparisonResult, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, Edge, ExplorationResult, ExploreOptions).
CLAUDE.md omits api/SketchHandlers.java, api/ExplorationHandlers.java, config/RelationType.java, utils/CqlUtils.java, utils/PatternSubstitution.java.
issues.suggestion: Regenerate the code-structure listing in CLAUDE.md from the actual source tree (e.g., `find src/main/java -name '*.java' | sort`). Remove the grammar/ entries, add the model/ package with all DTOs, correct the utils/PosGroup path, and list the two handler classes. Consider automating this section from a script to prevent future drift.

issues.summary: api/PatternSubstitution.java is a deprecated delegation-only wrapper that belongs to api/ (HTTP handlers), polluting its ownership boundary
evidence: api/PatternSubstitution.java carries @Deprecated and is a pure delegate to utils/PatternSubstitution (all three public methods forward directly).
The api package otherwise contains only HTTP handler classes (WordSketchApiServer, SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, HttpApiUtils) — CQL pattern utilities have no business there.
A parallel test file src/test/.../api/PatternSubstitutionTest.java exists alongside the deprecated class, meaning the zombie tests an already-moved concept.
issues.suggestion: Delete api/PatternSubstitution.java and its test file once SketchHandlers (the only remaining caller inside api/) is confirmed to import utils/PatternSubstitution directly (it already does). Verify no external callers reference the api-package version before deletion.

issues.summary: Multi-seed collocate-intersection logic is inlined in ExplorationHandlers rather than owned by SemanticFieldExplorer
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti() directly calls executor.executeSurfacePattern() in a loop and manually computes commonCollocates via retainAll — all domain logic inlined in an HTTP handler.

SemanticFieldExplorer owns compareCollocateProfiles() (used by handleSemanticField) and exploreByPattern() (used by handleSemanticFieldExplore), but handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti bypasses SemanticFieldExplorer entirely.

The class-level Javadoc on SemanticFieldExplorer mentions 'Comparison Mode (multi-seed)' and 'See {@link #compareSeeds}' but no compareSeeds method exists — evidence that the intended design had multi-seed logic in the explorer but it was never completed.
A developer changing multi-seed scoring logic must know to look in both ExplorationHandlers (actual implementation) and SemanticFieldExplorer (where the method should live) — ownership is split.
issues.suggestion: Add a compareSeeds(Set<String> seeds, RelationConfig relation, int top, double minLogDice) method to SemanticFieldExplorer that encapsulates the per-seed query loop and set-intersection logic. Reduce handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti to a thin HTTP adapter that calls that method and serialises the result, matching the pattern established by handleSemanticFieldExplore.

issues.summary: README documents GET /api/bcql but the server registers it as a POST-only endpoint
evidence: README.md line 119 lists '  GET /api/bcql' in the server startup endpoint table.
WordSketchApiServer.java uses server.createContext("/api/bcql", ...) (not registerGetHandler) and the startup logger prints 'POST /api/bcql - Execute BCQL query' at line 159.
The handler reads a JSON body (POST semantics); a GET request would receive no body.
issues.suggestion: Correct the README endpoint table to show 'POST /api/bcql' instead of 'GET /api/bcql'. Optionally consolidate the startup log entry with the README to prevent future drift.

root_causes: documentation_drift: Architecture documentation (CLAUDE.md, README) was not updated as the codebase evolved — the grammar/ package was replaced and the model/ package was added, but docs were not refreshed.

incomplete_migration: api/PatternSubstitution moved to utils/ but the deprecated wrapper was never deleted; multi-seed exploration was partially factored out but the domain logic remained inlined in the handler.
likely_symptoms: readme_endpoint_method_mismatch is symptom-level — the underlying cause is documentation_drift.
deprecated_zombie_in_wrong_package and multiseed_logic_bypasses_explorer both stem from the same incomplete_migration root cause.
